title: Escrow balances are formatted through Number(), losing bigint precision
reasoning: The contract amount is a bigint, but the dashboard converts it to a JavaScript Number before dividing by 1e18. JavaScript Numbers cannot exactly represent large integer token amounts, so the displayed BDAG balance can be rounded incorrectly for common on-chain values. This is user-visible accounting information on the dashboard, even if it does not alter the on-chain value.
reproduction: Render a dashboard escrow with a totalAmount bigint above Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER and near a 4-decimal rounding boundary; the value shown by Number(totalAmount) / 1e18 can differ from a bigint-safe formatter such as viem formatEther.
recommendation: Format token amounts with a bigint-safe utility, for example viem's formatEther/formatUnits followed by controlled decimal trimming, instead of converting the raw wei bigint to Number first.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The feature lists no linked tests, and the included files do not contain any dashboard rendering test for large bigint escrow totals.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a dashboard component/unit test that supplies a large bigint totalAmount and asserts the displayed BDAG value matches a bigint-safe formatUnits/formatEther result.
minimumFixScope: Update the dashboard amount formatting path for myEscrows and add a focused regression test for bigint token amount display.
